blessings of this Gathering I think this is much better than what we have to say and what you have to listen and may Allah subhana inshallah bring to our hearts and our minds and our families these blood and because of that they are Divine people and they would make other average human beings to worship them and the average human being had to consider the Emperor as God or had to consider all of those Royals from the emperor's family or the King's family had to consider them as Divine people so you see a great difference racial difference between the rulers and the average human beings now let's take a look at their social problems the royal families they did this but also average families in the society they would get married the father with his own daughter the brother with his own sister this was normal had there was no problem especially within the royal families why because they wanted to preserve the wealth and they wanted to preserve their Kingdom or the Empire these were social illnesses that existed back in those days to keep their blood pure to keep their Divine blood and then we go to and I'm not going to stop a lot and go into into into their problems but I just of God proven to be a messenger of God not just by words but with facts so the Hindus they believed also that they were divine and everybody else was their servant in India Hindus they believed that they are Divine and everybody else is their servant they had to serve these Divine leading families politically you know the leading whether they were kingdoms or just other types of governments they considered themselves as I said again Divine people look at the Arab Society or think about that before Prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam came there what were their problems major problems little we are the ones who give you and we are the ones who provide for them as well there's no reason for you to go and kill the children again Society disease what else a woman a woman would go with so many people as a matter of fact there's of she talks about four types of marriages and amongst these types of marriages he mentions and says that a woman would go with so many men and then if she had a child with this man she would point the finger at one of these man and would say you are my husband and the man had to accept that this is how the tradition was back in the days if we talk about they play over their m Muslims gambling yeah we know we don't win anything but just to kill the time like they have a lot to live Subhan Allah the time can be can be used in different ways not to kill the time to go to gamble in in Vegas so it is a disease it is a disease but look in what extent they went back in the days if they had no more money to to spend on gambling they would put their own families including children and their own houses when they would gamble so the weth finished then next step is putting the money I mean putting the house their own wives and their own children take it it's yours this is oppression this is real this is the real face of Oppression social oppression Prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam came with a Divine message brothers and sisters even average portrays the Muslims nowadays they so we know that a lot lot of them they so we know that we know that this is not the true thing we know that they they're trying to make the money they have their own benefit in this so we have to as Muslims though we have to recognize that Allah subhah wa ta'ala has sent the messenger of God as a true Mercy for for this world by the way Prophet Muhammad peace be upon him was not a mercy only for one kind of the world he was not Mercy only for the man he was not Mercy only for his close friends he was not Mercy only for the Arabs but he was Mercy for non-arabs he was Mercy for women he was Mercy for children he was Mercy even for little for animals the man who saved a dog or a woman who saved the cat earned the paradise.

summary: This is a lecture given at the MCCT First Annual Mawlid Celebration discussing the significance of Prophet Muhammad's arrival in the world. The speaker provides historical context about social problems in pre-Islamic societies, including oppression in the Byzantine and Persian empires, immoral social practices, slavery, infanticide, alcoholism, and gambling. He connects these historical problems to modern-day issues and emphasizes that the Prophet Muhammad came as a mercy for all of humanity—regardless of gender, ethnicity, or social status—bringing divine guidance to reform these societal diseases. The lecture concludes by highlighting the universal benefits of Islam and the Prophet's comprehensive message.
suggested_title: The Mawlid: Prophet Muhammad as a Mercy to All Humanity - Addressing Pre-Islamic Society's Problems
catchy_title: How Prophet Muhammad Healed a Broken World
